name: Ts. Abdul Rahman bin Mat
summary: Abdul Rahman Bin Mat is a senior lecturer for Software Engineering programme at the Faculty of Computer Science & Information Technology, Universiti Malaysia Sarawak (UNIMAS). He received his Masters in Computer Science and Engineering in Aizu University (Japan) at year 2005. He had more than 10 years’ experience in research and development (R&D). The area of interests includes requirement analysis and specification, knowledge-based system, formal method, and software testing.

name: Dr Amelia Jati Anak Robert Jupit
summary: Amelia Jupit is a senior lecturer at the Faculty of Computer Science and Information Technology at Universiti Malaysia Sarawak (UNIMAS). She earned her Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science (Hons) with a major in Multimedia Computing from UNIMAS in 2003 and a Master of Information Technology from the University of New South Wales in 2007. In 2009, she received her Postgraduate Diploma in Teaching and Learning. She completed her Ph.D. study on player's projective identity in digital games at the University of York. Her research interests include player experience, human-computer interaction, game-based learning, narrative, and identity.

name: Profesor Madya Dr. Cheah Wai Shiang
summary: Dr Cheah Wai Shiang is a senior lecturer at Faculty of Computer Science and Information Technology, Universiti Malaysia Sarawak, UNIMAS. He graduated with Master of Network Computing, Monash University, Australia and PhD in Software Engineering from The Melbourne University, Australia in year 2010. In year 2014-2016, he pursued his postdoctoral at Utrecht University and VU Amsterdam, Netherlands. He is a certified android developer.  His main research interest is dealing with agent in real time environment specially agent technology and games AI and validating agent oriented modelling (AOM). Also, he is interested on sustainability analysis of ICT4D projects through e3Value and blockchain and AI for community.

name: Profesor Ts. Dr. Dayang Nurfatimah bt Awg Iskandar
summary: Dayang NurFatimah Awg Iskandar is a professor at the Faculty of Computer Science and Information Technology (FCSIT), UNIMAS, Sarawak. She obtained her Bachelor degree (Hons) in Information Technology (majoring in Computational Science) from UNIMAS. She holds a Master in Multimedia Computing from Monash University, Australia and a PhD degree in Computer Science from Royal Melbourne Institute of Technology (RMIT), Australia. She pursued her postdoctoral studies with the Biomedical Informatics Systems Engineering Laboratory (BISEL), Heriot-Watt University, UK, working under the EU funded project -- Combining and Uniting Business Intelligence with Semantic Technologies (CUBIST). Dayang has expertise in spatio-temporal image analysis, semantic representation and retrieval, her research focuses on AI for minimising the gap between image features and high-level semantics in the domain of medicine, agriculture and astronomy. She was attached as a visiting academic researcher at the Jodrell Bank Centre for Astrophysics, within the Department of Physics and Astronomy at The University of Manchester, where she contributed to several cutting-edge research projects. Recently, she extended her professional expertise by serving with Petroleum Sarawak Berhad (PETROS), under the CEO@Faculty Programme.

summary:
Dr Edwin Mit is a Associate Professor at the Faculty of Computer Science and Information Technology, UNIMAS. He hold a BSc (Hons) in Computer Science, 1994 (UKM), MSc (Dist) in Software Engineering, 1998 (Bradford, UK), and PhD in Software Engineering, 2008 (Salford, UK), specialize in the area of formal methods software engineering.

name: Profesor Madya Dr. Halikul bin Lenando
summary: Associate Professor Dr. Halikul Lenando has served Universiti Malaysia Sarawak for almost 20 years as an academician. He has been appointed in the faculty management as Deputy Dean, Head of Department, and Progamme Coordinator. He obtained his PhD in Mobile Communication discipline from Cardiff University, United Kingdom in June 2012. His research interest is in mobile communication networks specializing in the communication protocols and data dissemination performance in opportunistic networks. Currently, he is actively conducting research in mobile sensor networks communication research group in his faculty. As to enrich his expertise, he has been appointed to be a fellow researcher of the Institute of Social Informatics and Technological Innovations (ISITI) under the Network Communication Infrastructure team.

name: Puan Hamizan binti Sharbini
summary: Hamizan binti Sharbini received her Bachelor Degree in Information and Communication Technology (ICT) from Universiti Tekonologi Petronas (UTP) Perak, Malaysia, in 2006. She then worked as a tutor in Faculty of Computer Science and Information Technology, Universiti Malaysia Sarawak in 2007. She furthered her study in Master degree majoring in Computer Science in 2009-2010 in Universiti Teknologi Malaysia (UTM), Johor, Malaysia. In 2010, she came back to the faculty and currently worked as lecturer. Her master thesis focused on crowd behavior modelling in panic situation using Social Force Model, with comparison with other existing models such as cellular automata, agent based model etc. Her research interest includes the area of crowd behavior modelling (hybrid model), optimization and artificial intelligent.

name: Puan Inson Binti Din
summary: Inson Din is an Information Systems lecturer in the Faculty of Computer Science and Information Technology, UNIMAS since 1997. Her areas of specialization are Database Design and Digital Marketing. She has served as a lecturer for more than 20 years and she realize that educating and teaching are her passion.  Be able to see the relieved and happy faces after they were so confused in understanding certain concepts such as normalization in Database subject really satisfy her. She also has embarked more in the projects that serve community especially in Digital Marketing. She is a Digital Marketing Certified Trainer  and really love to share her knowledge with the community in Sarawak or throughout Malaysia.

name: Profesor Madya Dr. Jacey Lynn Minoi
summary: Jacey-Lynn Minoi is a computer scientist, currently working at Universiti Malaysia Sarawak, where she is Lecturer of Computing and Exploratory Data Analysis. Her research focusses on the exploratory of the multivariate tensor-based and multivariate statistical shape analysis algorithms for shape modelling across multiple dimensions and for pattern recognition in fields of face analysis, face recognition and image processing applications. She heads the Biometric and Imaging group and Gamification group, whereby she is the driving force behind the computational  and exploratory thinking using gameful approach for building problem solving skills. Dr Minoi also often travel into the interiors of Sarawak, meeting and staying with the indigenous communities and empowering them with skills needed in her various projects on health informatics, aerial mapping using drones, fun learning, frugal innovation for climate change and community empowerment. These work have been made to national and international media attention.

summary: Dr Jane Labadin is a Professor at the Faculty of Computer Science and Information Technology as well as Research Fellow at the Institute of Social Informatics and Technological Innovation, Universiti Malaysia Sarawak (UNIMAS). She received her Ph.D. in Computational Mathematics specializing in Fluid Dynamics from the Imperial College of Science, Technology and Medicine, London, UK in 2002. Lau Sei Ping is a senior lecturer at Faculty of Computer Science & Information Technology (FCSIT), Universiti Malaysia Sarawak (UNIMAS). He received his Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) from University of Southampton, UK at 2015. 
His in-depth knowledge in computer science had given him the trust to handle wide varieties of course in UNIMAS including commercial postgraduate program courses. name: Profesor Madya Dr. Lee Nung Kion
summary:
I am interested in research topics related to Artificial Intelligence. I am also a digitalization consultant, and HRDC trainer in various Microsoft productivity tools, data analytics, and machine learning/artificial intelligence. 
My research concentrates on advancing knowledge in the following areas:

1.	Computer Vision:
My focus lies in leveraging computer vision techniques to enhance processes within the agriculture sector. I have also interest in using computer vision for social product innovation and communities’ development. 
2.	Machine Learning Algorithms for Complex Problems:
I specialize in designing and implementing machine learning algorithms model biological sequence signals, including genome data, protein sequences, and regulatory elements. 
3.	Deep Learning and Generative Artificial Intelligence:
My expertise extends to deep learning algorithms, which play a crucial role in improving classification tasks. Additionally, I am interested in generative artificial intelligence in solving real world problems. 
Doctorate and master’s students with a strong background in computer science are invited to connect with me. Feel free to drop me an email with a brief research topic. International students are welcome.

name: Profesor Dr. Narayanan A/L N Kulathu Ramaiyer
summary:
Professor Dr. Narayanan Kulathuramaiyer has served 24 years of his almost three decades in academia as one of the pioneer staff of Faculty of Computer Science and IT in UNIMAS. He is currently the Director of the Institute of Social Informatics and Technological Innovations (ISITI) and Professor of Computer Science at the Faculty of Computer Science and Information Technology, Universiti Malaysia Sarawak (UNIMAS). name: Dr Norfadzlan bin Yusup
summary: Dr. Norfadzlan bin Yusup is a Senior Lecturer at the Faculty of Computer Science and Information Technology (FCSIT), Universiti Malaysia Sarawak (UNIMAS). He obtained his Doctor of Philosophy (Computer Science) from Universiti Teknologi Malaysia (UTM). His research focuses on advancing nature-inspired computing, intelligent systems, and computational optimization, with applications spanning machining process optimization, sensor-based human activity recognition, and other emerging domains of intelligent computation. Passionate about data-driven intelligence, he integrates swarm-based optimization and machine learning techniques to advance feature selection, optimize classification performance, and improve predictive modeling efficiency.

name: Ts. Nurfauza bt Jali
summary:
Ts. Nurfauza Binti Jali is a Senior Lecturer at the Faculty of Computer Science and Information Technology, Universiti Malaysia Sarawak (UNIMAS), where she has served since 2001. She began her career as a Research Assistant, then spent two years as a Tutor before being appointed as a Lecturer in 2006. Nurfauza holds an MPhil in Computer Science from Queen’s University Belfast, an MSc in Real-Time Software Engineering from Universiti Teknologi Malaysia (UTM), and a BIT (Hons) in Software Engineering from Universiti Malaysia Sarawak (UNIMAS).

She has made significant contributions in research, teaching, and consultation. Her research spans key areas such as gamification to enhance software development performance, game-based learning to improve primary education, and mobile technology for community and industry projects. She has secured grants for multiple projects, including natural language processing techniques for goal model generation and integrating gamification into teaching. Nurfauza has also contributed to several publications, with topics ranging from UML model visualisation to software application development, demonstrating her depth of expertise in software engineering.

In teaching, Nurfauza has coordinated and taught a variety of undergraduate and postgraduate courses, focusing on programming, software requirements engineering, and web system development. She has actively promoted creativity and innovation in her teaching through the implementation of immersive learning strategies that prioritise real-world applications, particularly in software engineering in education, making learning experiences more engaging for students. 
Nurfauza provides consultancy in website and mobile app development, emphasizing Industry 4.0 initiatives. Her leadership bridges academic theory with practical applications, benefiting students and the community, demonstrating her significant contributions and fostering innovation in education and software engineering.

profile: Ts. Nurfauza Binti Jali is a Senior Lecturer at the Faculty of Computer Science and Information Technology, Universiti Malaysia Sarawak (UNIMAS), where she has served since 2001. She began her career as a Research Assistant, then spent two years as a Tutor before being appointed as a Lecturer in 2006. Nurfauza holds an MPhil in Computer Science from Queen’s University Belfast, an MSc in Real-Time Software Engineering from Universiti Teknologi Malaysia (UTM), and a BIT (Hons) in Software Engineering from Universiti Malaysia Sarawak (UNIMAS). She has made significant contributions in research, teaching, and consultation. Her research spans key areas such as gamification to enhance software development performance, game-based learning to improve primary education, and mobile technology for community and industry projects. She has secured grants for multiple projects, including natural language processing techniques for goal model generation and integrating gamification into teaching. Nurfauza has also contributed to several publications, with topics ranging from UML model visualisation to software application development, demonstrating her depth of expertise in software engineering. In teaching, Nurfauza has coordinated and taught a variety of undergraduate and postgraduate courses, focusing on programming, software requirements engineering, and web system development. She has actively promoted creativity and innovation in her teaching through the implementation of immersive learning strategies that prioritise real-world applications, particularly in software engineering in education, making learning experiences more engaging for students. Nurfauza provides consultancy in website and mobile app development, emphasizing Industry 4.0 initiatives. Her leadership bridges academic theory with practical applications, benefiting students and the community, demonstrating her significant contributions and fostering innovation in education and software engineering. name: Encik Rajan Thangaveloo
summary: Rajan Thangaveloo, CEM is a Senior lecturer at Faculty of Computer Science and Information Technology in Universiti Malaysia Sarawak (UNIMAS). He obtained his MSc in Network Systems Engineering (2007) and B.Eng (Hons) in Computer Engineering (2006) both from University of Plymouth, UK. His research interests include Computer Security, Network Security and Mobile Security. He has conducted undergraduate and postgraduate courses on Network and Security related courses since 1998. He is also a Certified Energy Manager (CEM). So far, he has published 2 book chapter and 9 papers in journal and conferences. He is leading 1 research grant and member of 3 other research grant.

name: Profesor Madya Dr. Stephanie Chua Hui Li
summary: Dr Stephanie Chua serves as an Associate Professor in the Faculty of Computer Science and Information Technology (FCSIT), Universiti Malaysia Sarawak (UNIMAS). She obtained her Ph.D from the University of Liverpool. Her research interests lie primarily in data, text and image mining. She is also interested in the areas of artificial intelligence and machine learning, applied data, text and image mining, exploratory data analysis and visualization and large language models. She has conducted research in text classification, text summarization, social media mining, frequent pattern analysis, sentiment analysis, genetic data mining, medical and epidemiological data analysis, medical image classification and other related application areas. She is also a Certified Data Science Specialist by iTrain Asia Pte Ltd, a Certified Tableau Desktop Specialist, a Gemini Certified Educator and a Google Certified Educator L1 & L2.

name: Dr Suhaila Binti Saee
summary: Suhaila Saee is a senior lecturer at the Faculty of Computer Science and Information Technology at University Malaysia Sarawak. She obtained her Bachelor of Computer Science in 2001 and Master of Computer Science in 2002 from Universiti Putra Malaysia, Malaysia. She later joined MIMOS Berhad, a leader of research and development (R&D) in ICT innovations in Malaysia, as a researcher at a Knowledge Engineering Research Lab and she was also involved in a Machine Translation Project. To continue her working experiences obtained from MIMOS, Professor Dr Alvin Yeo Wee and her had established a language technology research group known as Sarawak Language Technology (SaLT) in 2007. The research group is focusing mainly on digitisation and preservation of 63 Sarawak indigenous languages by applying computational linguistics and natural language processing techniques. Her research areas include Computational Linguistics (Computational Morphology) and Natural Language Processing (NLP). The area of interest is preserving under-resourced languages through speech and text processing.

name: Prof Madya Ts Dr Suriati Khartini Binti Jali
summary:
Suriati Khartini Jali is currently an associate professor at Faculty of Computer Science and Information Technology as well as Deputy Director at the Institute for Tourism Research and Innovation (ITRI), Universiti Malaysia Sarawak (UNIMAS). She obtained her PhD in Computing from the Serious Games Institute, Faculty of Engineering and Computing, Coventry University, UK.  She received her BSc (Multimedia) (Hons) from Universiti Putra Malaysia (UPM). Later, pursued her studies at the University of Bristol and rewarded with degree of MSc in Advanced Computing: Global and Multimedia Computing. She is also a registered trainer of the Human Resources Development Fund (HRDF). Her research interest focuses on the area of Serious Game, Game-based Learning (AR/VR), Web/Mobile-based design and application, eLearning, Human-Centered Computing, User Interaction (UI) and User Experience (UX).

More details at https://sites.google.com/view/suriatikhartinijali/

name: Ts. Syahrul Nizam bin Junaini
summary: Ts. Syahrul Nizam Junaini is a Senior Lecturer at the Faculty of Computer Science and Information Technology, Universiti Malaysia Sarawak (UNIMAS), and a Research Fellow at the Data Science Center and Centre for Spatially Integrated Digital Humanities (CSIDH),UNIMAS. Formerly the Deputy Director (Learning Technology) at the Centre for Applied Learning and Multimedia, he is a distinguished academic recognized nationally. He is a recipient of the Malaysia National Academic Award (2017), Sarawak Maal Hijrah Award (2018) and Malaysia Digital Transformation Award (2024). He is a member of National AI Office (NAIO) working group for AI talent and an expert member for Strategic AI Group for Education (SAGE-AI), UNIMAS. He published his articles in top-tier Q1 journals such as Journal of Cleaner Production, Education and Information Technologies and Agricultural Systems. He has been invited as a keynote speaker at international conferences in India, Japan, China, and Indonesia.

name: Dr Sze Jeeu Fong
summary: Dr Sze Jeeu Fong is a lecturer at the Faculty of Computer Science and Information Technology, Universiti Malaysia Sarawak (UNIMAS). She received her Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) in Operational Research from University of Kent, United Kingdom in year 2017. Her area of interests includes vehicle routing problems, scheduling problems, heuristic and metaheuristic methods. She is an active reviewer for a number of high indexed international journals and has also published in a few international ISI and Scopus journals.

name: Profesor Dr. Tan Chong Eng
summary:
Professor Chong Eng TAN has been a faculty member at the Faculty of Computer Science and Information Technology (FCSIT) at Universiti Malaysia Sarawak (UNIMAS) since 1999. He earned his Ph.D. from the University of Cambridge in 2004, specialising in wireless network and broadband access technology. 

With more than 25 years of expertise in wireless and network research, he has committed his endeavours to devising, innovating, and implementing solutions for rural Information and Communication Technology (ICT) development. In 2010, he successfully implemented the inaugural long-range wireless system in Bario and assumed the role of chief ICT architect for the TPOA Telecentre across four remote Orang Asli sites in West Malaysia. His notable research accomplishments encompass the Virtual Telecentre concept, power self-sufficient ICT design, and modular solar power system. As a distinguished senior member of the Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE), he actively engages with leading professional organisations, staying abreast of the latest developments in his field. 

He presently serves as the director of the Institute of Social Informatics and Technological Innovations (ISITI) at UNIMAS, concurrently leading the 5G & Connectivity Keylab under the auspices of the Sarawak Digital Economy Corporation (SDEC).

name: Profesor Madya Dr. Kartinah Bt Zen
summary:
Kartinah Zen is an Associate Professor at the Faculty of Computer Science and Information Technology (FCSIT), Universiti Malaysia Sarawak (UNIMAS). She obtained her Bachelor and Master in Computer Science, both from Universiti Malaya in 1996 and 2001 respectively. She holds a PhD in Engineering, specializing in Wireless Sensor Networks from Edith Cowan University, Perth, Australia in 2010. 
Her research interest is on embedded system, wireless sensor networks data transmission and sensor-related network technology and Internet of Things applications. She has successfully completed few research projects through government and industry funded grant. She is also a research fellow at the Centre of Excellence for Rural Informatics (ISITI), and currently working on project in wireless sensor network and Quality of Services (QoS) in Internet of Things (IoT). She has been involved in several research projects, including internal and external grant. She also has published several papers in her field and currently supervising few postgraduate students at Master and PhD level. 
Kartinah has served UNIMAS for more than 20 years. Along the services at UNIMAS, she has been given several higher academic administrator positions Currently, she holds the position as the Director of Centre for Flexible Learning (CFLEX).

She has been leading the Faculty as Dean from 2020-2024. Before stepping into this role, she was the Deputy Dean of Academics for six years. Kartinah is passionate about student development, especially in the fields of Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics (STEM). She initiated the Foundation in Computing program, offered at the Centre for Pre-University Studies (PPPU). 
Kartinah's positive and trustworthy leadership has elevated FCSIT to become a leading computing faculty in Borneo. Under her guidance, FCSIT received the Premier Digital Tech Institution (PDTI) award from the Malaysia Digital Economy Corporation (MDEC) in 2022.

name: Puan Rosita binti Mohamed Othman
summary:
Pn. Rosita received her Bachelor of Information Technology (Management Information Systems) from Universiti Kebangsaan Malaysia, Bangi and her Master of Computer Science (Software Engineering) from Universiti Putra Malaysia, Serdang, Selangor. Her area of interests is Knowledge Management and Educational Learning System. Currently, she is also involved with service learning curriculum and projects relating with requirement elicitation methods.

She has been teaching for more than 10 years and has joined the university workforce as a tutor. She is involved in systems’ design, development, entrepreneurial and management courses for undergraduates. Previously, Pn. Rosita was the Programme Coordinator for Information Systems Programme at Universiti Malaysia Sarawak for six years (2006-2012).

name: Prof Madya Ts Dr Sarah Flora Anak Samson Juan
summary: Sarah Samson Juan is the Deputy Dean of Industry and Community Engagement at the Faculty of Computer Science and Information Technology (FCSIT), Universiti Malaysia Sarawak (UNIMAS). She holds a Bachelor of Science in Industrial and Computational Mathematics from Universiti Malaya and a Master of Science and Technology with a specialization in Computation from the University of New South Wales, Australia. In 2015, she earned her Ph.D. in Computer Science from the University of Grenoble-Alpes, France. Her academic and research expertise lies at the intersection of mathematics and computer science, with research interests include mathematical modeling, natural language processing (NLP), and automatic speech recognition (ASR). During her Ph.D. studies, she made a significant contribution by publishing the first ASR dataset for the Iban language, which is now available through the OpenSLR database and GitHub (https://github.com/sarahjuan). Her current research is on machine learning models for processing Sarawak Malay dialect, code-switching social media data and news data with her postgraduate students. Furthermore, between 2021-2025, she has been involved in ESPERANTO Consortium, an international consortium led by Le Mans University that conducts speech technology research, funded by Marie Skłodowska-Curie Actions (MSCA) Staff Exchange program. Passionate about community education, she has actively engaged in digital literacy initiatives, training numerous teachers and students in block programming using Scratch, Hour of Code and PictoBlox. At FCSIT, she has taught undergraduate courses within the Computational Science program, including Artificial Intelligence, Mathematics for Computing, Discrete Mathematics, Differential Equations, and Mathematical Modeling and Simulation. At the postgraduate level, she has instructed courses in Research Methodology and Computational Thinking.
